description_long: This is a narrow, multi-story apartment building with a sloped, tiled roof and a facade made of light brown brickwork. The front features a mix of arched and rectangular windows, some with decorative brickwork, and two distinct entrances at street level, one with an arched doorway and the other with a rectangular frame. The side walls are plain brick with minimal detailing, and one side has a visible drainpipe running vertically. The rear of the building is a flat, featureless surface painted in a solid reddish color, lacking any windows or doors. The roof is covered with traditional terracotta tiles, giving the building a classic European appearance. The overall structure suggests a historical or traditional architectural style, likely found in older urban neighborhoods.
description: A narrow, multi-story brick apartment building with a sloped tiled roof, arched and rectangular windows, and two ground-level entrances. The facade is detailed, while the sides and rear are plain.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main facade with arched and rectangular windows, decorative brickwork, and two distinct entrances at the ground level.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a plain brick wall with a vertical drainpipe and no windows or doors.
description_view_2: The rear view is a flat, solid reddish surface with no windows, doors, or architectural details.
description_view_3: The right side view is similar to the left, showing a plain brick wall without any openings or significant features.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a mostly symmetrical vertical pattern, with a mix of arched and rectangular shapes grouped in pairs or singles across the facade.
